article_meta.title: Crisis Leadership: Using Technology To Maintain Stability And GrowthByPhil Portman,Forbes Councils Member.for

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several identifiable bias patterns in the author's own prose. This is an opinion/advertorial piece published as a 'Council Post' on Forbes, and while the article does contain a disclosure about opinions being those of the author, the author's prose itself contains multiple bias instances that should have been flagged. Key missed instances include: (1) The article reads as promotional content for automated communication tools (particularly SMS marketing, which is the author's own product) — this is Slant/Spin; (2) 'Using cutting-edge technologies, you can easily survive these VUCAP times' — this is an Unsubstantiated Claim and uses Subjective Qualifying Adjectives ('cutting-edge', 'easily'); (3) 'The best way to deal with VUCAP times is to use automated communication tools' — this is an Opinion Statement Presented as Fact; (4) The author is the CEO of Textdrip (an SMS marketing tool) and promotes automated communication tools without adequate disclosure of conflict of interest within the article body — Bias by Omission of conflict disclosure. However, one could argue this is clearly labeled as an opinion piece/council post and that some of these are expected in such a format. The empty array is still a notable miss.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: No attribution violations since the model returned an empty array — nothing was incorrectly flagged from quoted text.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No false positives since nothing was flagged at all.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Multiple clear bias instances were missed: 'cutting-edge technologies' (Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), 'you can easily survive' (Unsubstantiated Claims), 'The best way to deal with VUCAP times is to use automated communication tools' (Opinion Presented as Fact), and the overall slant toward the author's own product category without transparent disclosure. The article is essentially promotional content masquerading as thought leadership.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Cannot assess since no items were flagged.
